source_note: App-facing Codex index extracted from TechnoBioFasciaCodex v0.1, April 2026 sprint notes, Daily Practice Interface, Frequency Library, and next-pass corpus migration.
numbering_policy: Use stable app_id values for the Resonance Codex Companion while preserving source_plan_id for original TBFC planned-map fidelity.
numbering_warning: Original 45-entry TBFC map and later written-entry sequence diverge. Do not assume app_id equals original source table-of-contents number.

entries.title: Fascia: The Body's Undernet
entries.boundary_note: Anatomical and somatic-practice framing. Not diagnostic and not medical advice.
entries.core_thesis: Fascia is the continuous tensional network that gives every other system its shape, its address, and its voice.
entries.practice_gate: Fascial hydration: slow joint circles, warm breath, gentle tissue glide, no forcing.

entries.title: Mechanotransduction: How Touch Becomes Signal
entries.boundary_note: Biophysical concept with practice translation. Sensation logs are self-observation, not proof of cellular effect.
entries.core_thesis: Mechanical force becomes cellular information through receptors, cytoskeleton, and signaling cascades.
entries.practice_gate: 40 Hz orientation: brief soft vibration or palm pressure, then stillness to compare sensation.

entries.title: Myofascial Meridians: Where Anatomy Trains Meet Energy Lines
entries.boundary_note: Overlay map only. Anatomy Trains, meridian, chakra, and symbolic systems are not identical and should not be collapsed into one factual structure.
entries.core_thesis: Different body maps can be overlaid as navigational systems without pretending they are identical.
entries.practice_gate: Trace one body line slowly with attention, then journal the region that felt most available.

entries.title: Tensegrity: The Architecture of Aliveness
entries.boundary_note: Useful structural model and practice metaphor. Do not use as a diagnostic or universal explanation for pain.
entries.core_thesis: Living posture behaves less like stacked blocks and more like compression elements suspended in tension.
entries.practice_gate: Stand tall with soft knees; imagine bones floating inside a tension web.

entries.title: Extracellular Matrix: The Body's Living Scaffold
entries.boundary_note: Anatomical/scaffold framing with self-observation only. No treatment claims.
entries.core_thesis: The ECM is a biochemical and mechanical field surrounding cells.
entries.practice_gate: Gently lift and roll superficial skin/fascia where comfortable; compare texture before and after.

entries.title: Ground Substance: The Body's Liquid Crystal
entries.boundary_note: Hydration and tissue-state language is practice-facing. Do not imply verified tissue transformation from a single session.
entries.core_thesis: The gel-like matrix of fascia changes state with hydration, temperature, load, and time.
entries.practice_gate: Place a soft ball under a comfortable area for 60–90 seconds; wait for melting rather than forcing.

entries.title: Collagen Architecture: Cables of the Living Bridge
entries.boundary_note: Collagen hierarchy is anatomical/biophysical; slow-load practice is self-observation and not tissue remodeling advice.
entries.core_thesis: Collagen is not merely structural filler; it is the body's hierarchical cable system, scaling from molecular helix to whole-body force architecture.
entries.practice_gate: Apply a slow, low-force line stretch and track continuity without bouncing or forcing.

entries.title: Piezoelectricity of Bone and Connective Tissue
entries.boundary_note: Piezoelectricity is a real material principle; broad therapeutic claims require separate evidence and are not asserted here.
entries.core_thesis: Mechanical stress can generate electrical charge in collagen-rich and mineralized tissues, making the body's structural matrix also a signal-bearing material.
entries.practice_gate: Use gentle load-and-listen standing practice; treat sensation as observation, not proof of electrical effect.

entries.title: Fascial Innervation: The Sensory Web
entries.boundary_note: Fascial sensation can be tracked, but sensation logs are not diagnosis.
entries.core_thesis: Fascia is not passive wrapping; it is a richly innervated sensory field that helps the body detect pressure, stretch, vibration, pain, and position.
entries.practice_gate: Map light, medium, and sustained pressure sensations; stop if the signal becomes sharp or alarming.

entries.title: Interstitium: The Organ That Wasn't
entries.boundary_note: Use precise fluid-space and connective-tissue language. Avoid sensational hidden-organ or treatment claims.
entries.core_thesis: The interstitium reframes connective tissue as a fluid-filled continuity: not empty space between structures, but a distributed environment of flow, pressure, and exchange.
entries.practice_gate: Use fluid-continuity breathing and pressure awareness; observe without trying to force a result.
